positive.headline: The curious story of Britain's most famous WW2 poster
positive.summary: A forgotten Ww2 propaganda poster was rediscovered in a bookshop in Northumberland in 2000. The red poster and its bold, unadorned message beneath a Tudor crown, Keep Calm and Carry On, resonated with a world far removed from wartime Britain. It became one of the 21st century's most recognisable and repurposed cultural slogans.

positive.headline: The US town built by and for Chinese people
positive.summary: Locke is the only settlement in the Us developed by Chinese people for Chinese people. Locke was founded in 1848 by thousands of Chinese prospectors who flocked to the northern California foothills hoping to get rich. Today, some of the town's original descendants still live and run businesses in the 60 - person community.

Each glass dining cabin contains just one wooden table in order to keep the focus on the view.

positive.headline: Tea dance brought back to Kahkewistahaw First Nation after 80 years
positive.summary: The tea dance was outlawed under the Indian act until 1951. The ban aimed to assimilate first nations people by banning cultural practices. It's been 80 years since the community of Kahkewistahaw held a tea dance.

positive.headline: Attention stargazers! It's time for one of the best meteor showers of the year — the Perseids
positive.summary: This year, the moon will be roughly 84 per cent illuminated, which means it will wash out all but the brightest meteors. The shower runs from July 17 to August 23, but it peaks on the night of August 12 - 13.

neutral.headline: Inside Australia's billion-dollar bid to take on China's rare earth dominance

neutral.headline: 'Cryptocrash king'Do Kwon pleads guilty to fraud

Prosecutors say he arranged for a trading firm to surreptitiously buy millions of dollars worth of the token to restore TerraUSD's value. The following year, both TerraUSD and Luna collapsed, triggering a wider sell - off in the crypto market.

neutral.headline: Evacuations in Alaska after glacial melt raises fears of record flooding

neutral.headline: Trump's pick to lead economic data agency floats ending monthly jobs report

It is currently investigating how it handled historical allegations, including sexual assault and rape.

neutral.headline: Survival rates for most deadly cancers making little progress, experts warn
neutral.summary: Melanoma skin cancer 10 - year survival is now above 90 % in England and Wales. For all cancers half of patients can expect to live that long - double the 1970s figure. negative.headline: Spain wildfires are ‘clear warning’ of climate emergency, minister says
negative.summary: Fires have killed two people in Spain and forced thousands of people from their homes. Environment minister says the 14 wildfires still burning across seven spanish regions are proof of the country's vulnerability to global warming. Blazes in the Ourense province of Galicia have consumed 7, 000 hectares of land.

negative.headline: ‘It’s a robot war’: eastern Ukraine faces onslaught of Russian glide bombs, rockets and kamikaze drones
negative.summary: Over the past week, the Russian military deployed more than 1, 000 aerial bombs and nearly 1, 400 kamikaze drones against Ukraine. The current record is 728 drones and 13 missiles sent in a single night in July, most directed at the westerly part of the country. Since Donald Trump returned to the white house in January, the Kremlin has of Ukrainian cities including Kramatorsk.

negative.headline: At least 20 killed after boat capsizes off Italian island of Lampedusa, UN says
negative.summary: Rescuers have recovered 20 bodies so far and operations are continuing. Between 70 and 80 people were believed to have survived. The boat had been carrying 97 people when it overturned 14 miles south - west of Lampedusa.

negative.headline: Trump swallowing Putin’s lies is a bigger threat to Ukraine than bombs
negative.summary: President Obama promised to end the war within days of returning to the white House. The persistence of hostilities seven months after his inauguration is a rebuke to his self - image. Russia's industrial base and public opinion have been fired up for perpetual war. Any border or treaty that prevents the Kremlin dictating Ukraine's strategic orientation is illegitimate.

negative.headline: Police seize supercars worth more than £6m in crackdown on antisocial driving in London
negative.summary: Dozens of supercars collectively worth more than £ 6 m seized in operation targeting nuisance driving in the west end. Police said the cars, including twin purple lamborghinis, were either unroadworthy or being driven illegally. Eight people were arrested for alleged offences including actual bodily harm, criminal damage, drug offences, theft and immigration breaches.

negative.headline: ‘We don’t want him’: Dance Against Vance protest at US vice-president’s Cotswolds holiday
negative.summary: About 100 people, mostly women, turned up for the ânot welcomeâ party. Placards criticised the policies and stances championed by the Us vice - president, . Who is enjoying a country break down the road.'Heâs simply not welcome here, â said therapist Sue moon, from nearby Chipping Norton.

negative.headline: Terms & conditions
negative.summary: These are our terms and conditions for use of the network, which you may access in several ways, including but not limited to the world wide web via, digital television, PDA, mobile phone and RSS feeds. You agree to be responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your passwords or other account identifiers which you choose and all activities under your account. By accessing the Guardian Site via a third party site, app, link or otherwise, you agree that you have read, understood and agreed to be bound by these terms and.

negative.headline: Palestinian man killed in West Bank by Israeli soldier backing up settlers
negative.summary: Thamin Khalil Reda Dawabsheh, 35, was shot in the town of Duma, south of Nablus. He was killed by an off - duty soldier who was accompanying"an Israeli civilian"he succumbed to his wounds after being shot by colonists during an attack on the village. The attack is part of a pattern of increased Israeli settler violence.

negative.headline: Turkiye slams Israel, Kurdish-led SDF for destabilising a recovering Syria
negative.summary: Foreign ministers of Turkiye and Syria signed a memorandum of understanding on military training and consultancy after talks in Ankara. The countries had been negotiating a comprehensive military cooperation agreement for months since Al - Assad ’s fall. Obama slammed the U. S. For its support of Iraq and Afghanistan.

negative.headline: Lebanon rejects foreign interference, president tells Iran official
negative.summary: The visit comes a week after the lebanese government ordered the army to devise plans by the end of 2025 to disarm the Iran - aligned armed group."It is forbidden for anyone. .. To bear arms and to use foreign backing as leverage,"said President Aoun.

negative.headline: Wildfires scorch Greece as heatwave grips parts of Europe
negative.summary: Strong winds, prolonged dryness and intense heat have worsened conditions.82 new fires were recorded across the country the previous day, a fire official says. The situation is particularly dangerous near the western city of Patras, where two large fires are burning.

negative.headline: Does a Trump-brokered deal squeeze Russia, Iran out of the South Caucasus?
negative.summary: U. S. President Donald Trump hosted a summit between azeri and armenian leaders. The two sides signed a preliminary peace deal to end the decades - long conflict over Nagorno - Karabakh. After winning the 2020 war over the region, Baku has been eager to revive the corridor. It could become a mammoth transport hub between Turkiye, Azerbaijan and central Asia.

negative.headline: Is Israel using Gaza tribal militias to help ethnic cleansing?
negative.summary: In June, Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu to arm and supporting the Popular Forces militia in Gaza to oppose Hamas. Experts believe Israel is backing the militia and its leader, Yasser Abu Shabab, to put a Palestinian face on the ethnic cleansing of Gaza. Despite his alleged affiliation with ISIL, analysts say it is using him to advance its ethnic cleansing plans in Gaza.

negative.headline: Will Trump’s India tariffs shut down world’s biggest cut diamond supplier?
negative.summary: A 25 percent reciprocal tariff on all Indian goods came into effect on August 7. Diamond exporters in India say orders have already started getting cancelled. U. S. President's tariff on imports from India could be final nail in the industry's coffin.

negative.headline: The view from Russia: Putin will take ‘symbolic steps’ during Trump talks
negative.summary: U. S. President Donald Trump and his Russian counterpart, Vladimir Putin, met in Alaska on Friday. The meeting has raised hopes among some Russians."This is no longer just diplomacy. It is a redrawing of the geopolitical map,"one resident said.

negative.headline: How a student’s death in Mexico raises questions about police violence
negative.summary: Jesus Alain Vasquez Perez was a student at a teacher training school in southern Mexico. He died after falling from a pick - up truck while protesters rallied for better access to education. Experts say his death underscores the distrust between police and some of the poorest students. The school is part of a nationwide network of government - funded normal schools.

negative.headline: Assaults by Israeli soldiers become a daily reality in West Bank’s Sebastia
negative.summary: Villagers say increasing attacks are part of Israel's plan to drive Palestinians out. Israel has been eyeing Sebastia's significant archaeological site since 2023. Unemployment soared over 30 percent, leaving family without regular work.

negative.headline: Forcing Tests could ‘bankrupt’ cricket countries: Australia chief
negative.summary: Cricket chief Todd Greenberg says a slimmer Test calendar could benefit smaller nations. Greenberg suggests that quality, not quantity, was important in keeping the red - ball game viable. The five - day game remained in rude health in Australia, where interest in the ashes has been"off the charts".

negative.headline: South Sudan denies Israel talks over forced transfer of Palestinians
negative.summary: The Associated press reported that the two countries were engaged in talks over an Israeli proposal to forcibly transfer Palestinian residents from Gaza to south Sudan. South Sudan says the claims are"baseless and do not reflect the official position or policy"a number of Israeli officials have proposed the forced displacement of Palestinians from Gaza. Human rights groups warn a mass departure could pave the way for Israel to annex the enclave.

negative.headline: Researchers warn that our increasing use of AI could be limiting our language and thought processes
negative.summary: More than half of text online is now likely generated in full or in part by AI, experts say. A recent study found automated traffic surpassed human - generated activity online for the first time in 2024. Some worry this is creating a feedback loop that could shrink human creativity.

negative.headline: At least 123 Palestinians killed as Israeli army pounds Gaza City ahead of planned takeover
negative.summary: The 24 - hour death toll is the worst in a week."They're not being pushed out, they'll be allowed to exit,"prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu says. A ceasefire is still possible, though talks have been floundering. Eight more people, including three children, have died of starvation and malnutrition in Gaza.

negative.headline: Mountie abused role when he started relationship with complainant during investigation: disciplinary board
negative.summary: A Nova Scotia Mountie abused his role when he pursued an intimate sexual relationship with the complainant in an assault case, according to an RCMP code of conduct board ruling."Police officers have a duty to protect vulnerable people. [ he ] failed to do that,"the adjudicator wrote in her decision. The relationship between the mountie and the woman dates back to 2015.

negative.headline: It's been a busy month for Trump's retribution campaign
negative.summary: A grand jury has convened and subpoenaed Letitia James'office, media outlets report. A prosecutor says the probe is a"desperate example"of the administration's"political retribution campaign"it's unclear if the probes will lead to actual legal peril or if they are simply a means to make their lives miserable.
